visibility: Antarctica, parts of Africa, southern Atlantic
notes: Ring of fire annular eclipse. Path of annularity crosses Antarctica.
Total lunar eclipse with blood moon. Duration of totality ~58 min.

visibility: Greenland, Iceland, Spain, parts of Portugal
notes: Major total solar eclipse. Totality up to 2m 18s. First total solar eclipse visible from Spain since 1905.

Ring of fire crosses Chile, Argentina, Atlantic.
Very minor partial (~3%).

visibility: North Africa, Middle East, Saudi Arabia
notes: The 'Great North African Eclipse' — longest total solar eclipse until 2114, up to 6m 23s of totality. Major astronomical event.
Small partial lunar eclipse (~4%).

visibility: South America, Atlantic, Europe, West Africa
notes: Annular path through South America and Spain/Portugal.
Totality crosses Sydney, Australia. Up to 5m 10s of totality.
New Year's Eve blood moon. ~1h 11min of totality.
Partial solar eclipse up to 87% obscuration.
Partial solar eclipse up to 46%.
Dramatic total lunar eclipse, ~1h 42min totality.
Partial solar eclipse.

Total lunar eclipse ~54min totality.

visibility: North Africa, Europe, Russia, North Pacific
notes: Annular path through Algeria, Greece, Turkey, Russia.

visibility: Botswana, South Africa, Indian Ocean, Australia
notes: Total solar eclipse. Totality up to 3m 44s across southern Africa and Australia.
